summary: Verified cookie-auth migration end-to-end. Backend: 11 new cookie-specific tests + full 44-test regression all pass (55/55). Set-Cookie sets httpOnly+Secure+SameSite=None for access_token and refresh_token; /auth/profile works via cookie-only and via Bearer-only; /auth/refresh works with empty body (cookie) and with {refresh_token} body; /auth/logout clears cookies and a subsequent /profile returns 401; protected routes & RBAC still enforced via cookie auth; seed data intact (33 products, 6 cats, 8 suppliers, low/out_of_stock present); dashboard shape preserved post-refactor (kpi+4 chart arrays). Frontend: login at /login redirects to /dashboard with no tokens in localStorage; httpOnly+Secure cookies set by browser; hard reload keeps session (cookie persistence via bootstrap /auth/profile); UI Log out via user-menu dropdown redirects to /login; /dashboard requires auth (redirects to /login after logout); staff sidebar correctly hides admin sections (Categories, Suppliers, Audit Trail).

issues: Console shows 4 x 'Failed to load resource: 401' and 2 x 'Token refresh failed' on first load — caused by /auth/profile bootstrap firing while no cookie exists, then the 401 interceptor attempting /auth/refresh which also 401s. Functionally harmless but noisy in DevTools. Consider skipping bootstrap profile call if we know we're on /login, or silencing the interceptor's console.error for the unauthenticated bootstrap case.

action_items: (Optional) Silence the /auth/profile 401 + 'Token refresh failed' console noise on unauthenticated initial loads (skip refresh attempt on bootstrap or when path is /login).

(Carry-over from iter_1) auth.py clear_auth_cookies should mirror the same secure flag as set_auth_cookies (currently hard-codes secure=True while set uses env COOKIE_SECURE). Fine for current prod (https) but inconsistent for local-http dev.
(Carry-over) CORS uses allow_origin_regex='.*' with allow_credentials=True — works with current single-origin preview but is generally discouraged; consider explicit origins list for production.
(Carry-over) /auth/login still has no brute-force lockout/rate-limit (auth playbook expectation).
critical_code_review_comments: clear_auth_cookies in auth.py hardcodes secure=True; set_auth_cookies reads COOKIE_SECURE env. Use the same env-driven flag in both for consistency.

refresh endpoint reads request body via await request.json() and swallows any exception — fine, but if a non-JSON body is posted (e.g., empty Content-Length) FastAPI's Request.json() can throw; the broad except keeps it safe.

set-cookie uses SameSite=None which REQUIRES Secure (already set). Works only over https — documented in review request; not portable to plain-http local dev unless COOKIE_SECURE=false is set AND samesite changes to 'lax'.

Frontend api.js refresh-on-401 retries the original request without checking the refresh response status; if /auth/refresh returns 200 with empty cookie (edge case) the retry will loop only once due to _retry flag — acceptable, but consider also short-circuiting when refresh returns 401.

AuthContext.jsx bootstrap calls /auth/profile unconditionally; on first visit (no cookie) this generates expected 401 + a refresh attempt that also 401s. Consider gating on a hint cookie or stored 'has_session' flag to reduce noise.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows (login, no-localStorage tokens, httpOnly cookies set, hard-reload persistence, UI logout, protected route, RBAC sidebar)

seed_data_creation: Cookie tests create no persistent TEST_-prefixed data. Regression test_cookie_auth.py + backend_test.py share the existing TEST_* cleanup path.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Cookie auth fully verified. Logout selector in UI: open '[data-testid="user-menu"]' dropdown then click text 'Log out' (two words, exact match). NOTE: /app/memory/test_credentials.md still documents the OLD localStorage strategy — update it to reflect httpOnly cookies. Backend cookies use SameSite=None+Secure so require https. Run all backend tests with: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v
rca of the issue: No bugs — migration is correct. Only nit: noisy console 401s on first visit to /login due to AuthContext bootstrap firing /auth/profile then retrying via /auth/refresh. Functionally correct (catches 401 silently for auth bootstrap path), but the interceptor still console.error's the refresh failure.
